description: Lecture 19 discusses revenue maximization in multi-parameter settings, building on Myerson's theory of optimal auctions. The lecture explores the complexities of achieving revenue maximization and introduces linear programming as a tool for auction design. Key concepts include the need for randomization in auctions and the independence of bidders' valuations.
topic_summary: This lecture focuses on extending Myerson's single-parameter auction theory to multi-parameter settings, emphasizing the challenges and the role of linear programming in auction design. The discussion includes the importance of randomization and the independence of bidders' valuations.
